ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.
M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported
e.g., Baseline, 6-Month Follow Up, Year 2, Daily

ElementDescription: Have you ever been tested for HIV?
Notes: 0 = No; 1 = Yes; 7 = Refused to answer; 9 = Don't Know

ElementDescription: What month (1-12) did you have your most recent HIV test?
What year did you have your most recent HIV test?
What was the result of your most recent HIV test?
Notes: 1 = Negative; 2 = Positive; 3 = Never obtained results; 4 = Indeterminate; 7 = Refused to answer; 9 = Don't Know

ElementDescription: LAST HIV TEST WAS DONE (Check one):
Notes: 1 = < 12 months ago; 2 = > 12 months ago; 3 = Don't Know/Refused

ElementDescription: I'm going to read you a list of reasons why some people have not been tested for HIV. Which of these best describes the most important reason you have not been tested for HIV in the past 12 months?
Notes: 1 = You think you are at low risk for HIV infection; 2 = You were afraid of finding out that you had HIV; 3 = You didn't have time; 4 = Some other reason; 5 = No particular reason; 7 = Refused to answer; 9 = Don't Know
